Legal Information

Complete legal documentation for the HostingCo system including licensing, patents, and third-party attributions.

Legal Overview

The HostingCo system is released under the MIT License with additional patent rights granted to users. This document provides comprehensive legal information about the software, including licensing terms, patent rights, third-party attributions, and contributor information.

Important: This legal information applies to the entire HostingCo system including the frontend application, backend API, documentation, and all associated components.

License

The HostingCo system is licensed under the MIT License, which provides:

  • Commercial Use: Use the software for commercial purposes
  • Modification: Modify the software to suit your needs
  • Distribution: Distribute copies of the software
  • Sublicensing: License your derivative works under the same terms
  • Private Use: Use the software privately without disclosure

License Conditions

The MIT License requires:

  • Inclusion of the original copyright notice and license text
  • Preservation of attribution notices in the source code
  • Disclosure of significant modifications to the original software
Limitation: The software is provided "AS IS" without warranty of any kind. The authors and copyright holders disclaim all liability for damages arising from the use of this software.

Patent Rights

In addition to the MIT License, HostingCo provides a Patent Grant that:

  • Grants a perpetual, worldwide, non-exclusive patent license
  • Covers patent claims necessarily infringed by your use of the software
  • Includes defensive termination provisions
  • Applies to all contributions submitted to the project

Defensive Termination

The patent license includes a defensive termination clause that terminates patent rights if you initiate patent litigation against HostingCo or other users alleging that the software infringes your patents.

Covered Patents

The patent license covers claims related to:

  • Hosting management system architecture
  • Automated server provisioning and deployment
  • Real-time monitoring and alerting systems
  • Customer support ticket management
  • Billing and payment processing integration
  • Multi-tenant user management systems
  • Database schema and data management
  • API design and implementation
  • Security authentication and authorization systems
  • Configuration management systems

Third-Party Attributions

The HostingCo system incorporates numerous third-party open source components. The NOTICE file provides complete attribution for all external dependencies.

Major Dependencies

Frontend Technologies

  • React - MIT License
  • TypeScript - Apache License 2.0
  • Vite - MIT License
  • Tailwind CSS - MIT License
  • Inter Font - SIL Open Font License 1.1

Backend Technologies

  • Node.js - MIT License
  • Express.js - MIT License
  • PostgreSQL - PostgreSQL License
  • Redis - BSD 3-Clause License
  • Knex.js - MIT License

Infrastructure & Deployment

  • Docker - Apache License 2.0
  • Nginx - BSD 2-Clause License
  • GitHub Actions - MIT License

License Directory

All third-party license files are available in the legal/licenses/ directory, including:

  • MIT License
  • Apache License 2.0
  • BSD 3-Clause License
  • ISC License
  • SIL Open Font License 1.1

Contributors

The HostingCo system is made possible by the contributions of numerous individuals and organizations. The CONTRIBUTORS.md file acknowledges all contributors.

Core Team

  • HostingCo Development Team - Architecture, design, and implementation
  • Frontend Team - React/TypeScript application development
  • Backend Team - Node.js/Express API development
  • DevOps Team - Docker deployment and CI/CD pipelines
  • Security Team - Authentication, authorization, and security implementation

Community Contributors

  • Code Contributors - Pull requests and code improvements
  • Documentation Contributors - Technical writing and documentation
  • Design Contributors - UI/UX design and accessibility improvements
  • Testing Contributors - Bug identification and quality assurance
  • Community Support - Issue triage and user assistance

How to Contribute

If you would like to contribute to the HostingCo system, please see our Contributing Guidelines for detailed information on:

  • How to submit pull requests
  • Code of conduct
  • Development process
  • Issue reporting
  • Feature requests

Compliance Information

HostingCo maintains strict compliance with all applicable open source licenses and legal requirements.

License Compliance

  • Attribution: All third-party components are properly attributed
  • License Preservation: Original license files are included
  • Source Availability: Source code is available under the MIT license
  • Patent Rights: Patent rights are granted as specified

Security Compliance

  • Vulnerability Disclosure: Security issues are handled responsibly
  • Encryption Standards: Industry-standard encryption is used
  • Privacy Compliance: Data protection regulations are followed
  • Audit Logging: Comprehensive audit trails are maintained

Export Compliance

The HostingCo system is subject to export control laws and regulations. Users are responsible for ensuring compliance with applicable export laws in their jurisdiction.

Legal Contact Information

For questions about licensing, patents, third-party attributions, or legal compliance:

HostingCo Legal Department
Email: legal@hostingco.com
GitHub Issues
Report Issues
Contributor Questions
Email: contributors@hostingco.com

Reporting Issues

If you believe there are licensing issues, missing attributions, or other legal concerns, please:

  1. Open an issue on our GitHub repository
  2. Email our legal department directly
  3. Provide specific details about the concern
  4. Include relevant documentation or evidence
Response Time: We typically respond to legal inquiries within 5-7 business days. For urgent matters, please indicate the urgency in your communication.
